scope.task: Prepare corridor-specific cleanup recommendation for the sole remaining Church Avenue corridor duplicate.

recommendation.no_mutation_reason: Do not add an alias or do-not-merge override yet. The Bus Forward 2 SBS candidate record names only 'Church Avenue' with no limits, while corridor_church-av is explicitly Flatbush Ave to Ocean Pkwy. That blocks a high-confidence merge alias. However, the SBS candidate also lacks a conflicting extent, so it does not support a high-confidence do-not-merge guard against the Flatbush-to-Ocean-Pkwy corridor. The same source separately lists a Church Av local corridor from Coney Island Av to Flatbush Av, and that adjacent segment already has a DNM guard against corridor_church-av, but that guard should not be inferred onto the unbounded SBS candidate without source-backed limits or a project policy.
needed_to_mutate_later: Exact source evidence that Bus Forward 2 candidate G Church Avenue maps to Flatbush Ave to Ocean Pkwy, which would support aliasing the SBS candidate shell to corridor_church-av.

Exact source evidence that Bus Forward 2 candidate G Church Avenue maps to a different bounded segment, or a durable corridor identity policy that treats unbounded SBS candidate shells as separate from later bounded project corridors, which would support a do-not-merge guard.

validator_issue.message: Global corridor records corridor_bus-fwd-2-church-av-brooklyn-sbs-candidate and corridor_church-av share identity key corridor_church-ave.

merge_patterns_considered.pattern: Sparse Bus Forward 2 candidate shells can be merged when the target has matching geography or lifecycle evidence and no conflicting extent.
merge_patterns_considered.why_not_enough: The Church Avenue SBS candidate has no limits and the target is a specific 0.9-mile segment; the same source also has a separate adjacent Church Av local segment.

do_not_merge_patterns_considered.pattern: Shared street name is insufficient when source-backed boroughs or limits show distinct physical corridors.
examples: data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json guards corridor_bus-fwd-2-church-av-brooklyn-local against corridor_church-av because Coney Island Av to Flatbush Av and Flatbush Ave to Ocean Pkwy are adjacent but distinct.
data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json guards Broadway records where boroughs and limits differ.
do_not_merge_patterns_considered.why_not_enough: The adjacent local Church Av segment has explicit limits, but the SBS candidate has no explicit limits. There is not enough conflict evidence to make a durable DNM guard for the SBS candidate.

prior_review_notes_considered.finding: Deferred this pair because the Bus Forward 2 SBS candidate is a sparse Church Avenue next-phase candidate without explicit limits, while corridor_church-av is the later Flatbush Avenue to Ocean Parkway priority corridor.
Deferred this pair again, noting that neither alias nor do-not-merge is high confidence without limit evidence or policy.
Marked the Church Avenue cluster low confidence because the unlabeled SBS candidate cannot be safely collapsed into the target without stronger extent evidence.

merge_alias_assessment.reason: A merge would treat the unbounded 2017 SBS candidate G as the same corridor as the 2019 Flatbush Ave to Ocean Pkwy project. Current source evidence does not explicitly connect candidate G to those limits, and the same Bus Forward 2 report separately identifies an adjacent Church Av local segment.

do_not_merge_assessment.reason: A DNM guard would assert the SBS candidate is distinct from Flatbush Ave to Ocean Pkwy. Current source evidence does not provide conflicting limits for the SBS candidate, only missing limits.

review_note_assessment.reason: The correct deterministic artifact is a low-confidence review note preserving the evidence and blocker until either source-backed limits or corridor-shell policy exists.
